zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud Green於1492年4月6日出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾格林也斯 諾頓時，她的父親Sir Thomas de Grene V of Grene's Norton31歲，母親Lady Johane Fogge of Repton23歲。她於1510年與Sir Thomas Parr在英國諾薩普頓史爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1531年12月1日在英國英格蘭倫敦享年39歲，埋葬在英國倫敦布拉可福利亞斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud Green于1492年4月6日出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾格林也斯 諾頓时，她的父亲Sir Thomas de Grene V of Grene's Norton31岁，母亲Lady Johane Fogge of Repton23岁。她于1510年与Sir Thomas Parr在英国諾薩普頓史爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1531年12月1日在英国英格兰倫敦享年39岁，埋葬在英国倫敦布拉可福利亞斯。

nameMeanings.name: Green
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: either a nickname for someone who was fond of dressing in this color (Old English grēne) or was young or immature, or who had played the part of the ‘Green Man’ in the May Day celebrations, or a topographic name for someone who lived near a village green (Middle English grene, a transferred use of the color term). This is one of the most common and widespread of English surnames. In North America it has assimilated cognates from other languages, notably German Grün (see Gruen ) and Dutch Groen ; compare 7 below. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: alternatively, from a Middle English personal name Grene.
Irish: adopted for Ó hUainín ‘descendant of Uainín’, a personal name from a pet form of uaine ‘green’, see Honan .

variants.name: Greenman
meanings: English (Wiltshire): from Middle English grene ‘green’ + man ‘man’, a nickname from the Green Man of folklore, or a topographic name for someone who lived by the village green. Compare Green .

Americanized form (translation into English) of German Grunemann or Grünemann: topographic name for someone who lived by the village green (compare above and Greenland ), or a habitational name for someone from any of numerous places called Grüna, Grünau, or Grüne.

variants.name: Greening
meanings: English (Gloucestershire and Dorset): nickname perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English grening, a derivative of grene ‘green’ (Old English grēne), i.e. ‘the green one’, whether physically or metaphorically ‘green’.
English: alternatively, from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Grening, a derivative (originally patronymic) of the Middle English personal name Grene. See Green .
Americanized form of German Grüning, a variant of Groening .

variants.name: Gruen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Dieter, Dietrich, Georg, Hans, Heinz, Hermann, Hertha.  Jewish Ehud, Eliezer, Isadore, Isidor, Meyer, Rivka, Shimon, Shlomo, Shoshana.

German (Grün): from Middle High German grūene ‘green, fresh, raw’, hence a nickname for someone who habitually dressed in green, a topographic name for someone who lived in a green and leafy place, or a habitational name for someone from a place called with this word, such as Gruna, Grunau in Silesia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; Grün): artificial name from German grün ‘green’. Compare Grun .

variants.name: Greene
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Green  . In North America, this surname has assimilated cognates from other languages.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud Green was born on 6 April 1492, in Green's Norton, Northamptonshire, England, her father, Sir Thomas de Grene V of Grene's Norton, was 31 and her mother, Lady Johane Fogge of Repton, was 23. She married Sir Thomas Parr in 1510, in Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 1 December 1531, in London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 39, and was buried in Blackfriars, London, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud Green est née le 6 avril 1492, à Green's Norton, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas de Grene V of Grene's Norton, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Lady Johane Fogge of Repton, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Parr en 1510, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 décembre 1531, à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterrée à Blackfriars, Londres, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud Green am 6. April 1492 in Green's Norton, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas de Grene V of Grene's Norton 31 und ihre Mutter Lady Johane Fogge of Repton 23. Sie hat 1510 in Northamptonshire, England Sir Thomas Parr geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. Dezember 1531 im Alter von 39 Jahren in London, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Blackfriars, London, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Green è nata il il 6 aprile 1492 nel Green's Norton, Northamptonshire, England, sua padre, Sir Thomas de Grene V of Grene's Norton, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Lady Johane Fogge of Repton, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Sir Thomas Parr nel 1510, nel Northamptonshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 1 dicembre 1531, nel London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 39 anni, e fu sepolta a Blackfriars, London, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud Greenが1492年4月6日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアグレエン'ス ノートンで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas de Grene V of Grene's Nortonは31歳，母Lady Johane Fogge of Reptonは23歳であった。1510年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアでSir Thomas Parrと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1531年12月1日にイギリスイギリスロンドンで39歳で死去し，イギリスロンドンブラクフリアルズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud Green(이)가 1492년 4월 6일 영국 노르섬프토실르 그린'스 노르턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas de Grene V of Grene's Norton(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Johane Fogge of Repton(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1510년 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 Sir Thomas Parr(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1531년 12월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 런던 블랙프리아르스에 묻혔다.

mn.6 April 1492–1 December 1531: 1492 оны Дөрөвдүгээр сарын 6–1531 оны Арванхоёрдугаар сарын 1

pt.6 April 1492–1 December 1531: 6 de abril de 1492–1 de dezembro de 1531
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Green nasceu em 6 de abril de 1492, em Green's Norton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas de Grene V of Grene's Norton, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Lady Johane Fogge of Repton, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas Parr em 1510, em Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de dezembro de 1531, em London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 39 anos, e foi sepultada em Blackfriars, London, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud Green родилась 6 апреля 1492 г. в местности грин'с нортон, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas de Grene V of Grene's Norton было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Johane Fogge of Repton, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Parr в 1510 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 1 декабря 1531 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 39 лет и была похоронена в местности блэккфрярс, Лондон, Англия.

es.6 April 1492–1 December 1531: 6 de abril de 1492–1 de diciembre de 1531
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud Green nació el 6 de abril de 1492, en Green's Norton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas de Grene V of Grene's Norton, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Lady Johane Fogge of Repton, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Sir Thomas Parr en 1510, en Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 1 de diciembre de 1531, en London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 39 años, y fue sepultada en Blackfriars, London, Inglaterra.
